name: Los Rubiales - Rio Turia (PR-CV 187) — Circular desde Aras de los Olmos
name_original: Los Rubiales - Río Turia (PR-CV 187) — Circular desde Aras de los Olmos
region_intro: Hiking around Aras De Los Olmos offers diverse terrain, characterized by central plains and predominantly mountainous areas with deep ravines. The region features a vast forest mass and is crossed by the Turia River and its tributaries, contributing to rich biodiversity. Elevations range from 500 meters along the Turia River to peaks of 1,200 meters, creating varied landscapes for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Aras De Los Olmos The most popular hiking route is Los Rubiales - Rio Turia (PR-CV 187) — Circular desde Aras de los Olmos, a 11.0 miles (17.8 km) difficult trail that takes about 5 hours 30 minutes to complete, offering a comprehensive journey through wooded and riverside areas. Another top favourite among local hikers is Water Trail, a moderate 8.9 miles (14.3 km) path that typically follows natural water features and takes around 4 hours. Local hikers also love the Arcos de las Salinas – Baños Waterfall loop from Arcos de las Salinas, a 2.2 miles (3.6 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 1 hour. Hiking around Aras De Los Olmos is defined by mountainous terrain, deep ravines, and the Turia River, with a network of routes offering options for different ability levels. The routes in Aras De Los Olmos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 11 reviews. More than 160 hikers have used komoot to explore Aras De Los Olmos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Aras De Los Olmos?
A: There are over 25 hiking trails in Aras De Los Olmos, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. The komoot community has explored more than 29 routes in the area.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Aras De Los Olmos?
A: Hikes in Aras De Los Olmos feature diverse terrain, ranging from central plains to predominantly mountainous areas with deep ravines. You'll find vast forest masses, the scenic Turia River and its tributaries, and elevations varying from 500 meters to peaks of 1,200 meters, creating a rich and varied landscape.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Aras De Los Olmos?
A: Yes, Aras De Los Olmos offers several easy hiking options. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40301447" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arcos de las Salinas – Baños Waterfall loop from Arcos de las Salinas&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 2.2-mile trail that can be completed in about an hour, leading through varied terrain.

Q: Can I find challenging hikes with significant elevation gain in the region?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a challenge, Aras De Los Olmos has several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e822504358/los-rubiales-rio-turia-pr-cv-187-circular-desde-aras-de-los-olmos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Rubiales - Rio Turia (PR-CV 187) — Circular desde Aras de los Olmos&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 11-mile trail with over 600 meters of elevation gain, offering a comprehensive journey through wooded and riverside areas.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes in Aras De Los Olmos?
A: Yes, many routes in Aras De Los Olmos are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e822504358/los-rubiales-rio-turia-pr-cv-187-circular-desde-aras-de-los-olmos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Rubiales - Rio Turia (PR-CV 187)&lt;/a&gt; is a notable circular route, and the Senda de las Áreas Recreativas is another medium-difficulty circular option connecting several recreational areas.

Q: Are there any trails that feature waterfalls or water elements?
A: Yes, the region is rich in water features. The Barranco Regajo trail is known for its small waterfalls and refreshing environment. You can also explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010570743/water-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Water Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 8.9-mile path that typically follows natural water features. The Turia River also creates scenic gorges like those of La Hoz, del Carril, and Regajo Spider.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I explore while hiking?
A: Aras De Los Olmos offers several unique natural attractions. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4599624" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dino Route of Aras de los Olmos&lt;/a&gt;, a natural monument with dinosaur footprints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1564504" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ancient juniper forest&lt;/a&gt;, part of the Ruta de la Travinas, allows you to walk among large, old juniper trees. For panoramic views, consider the demanding climb to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2306112" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Buitre&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4457154" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirar Bueno Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I go stargazing after a hike in Aras De Los Olmos?
A: Yes, Aras De Los Olmos is designated as a Starlight Reserve due to its commitment to preserving the quality of its night sky. This makes it an exceptional location for stargazing, complementing your daytime hiking activities. The municipality even hosts astronomical observatories.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Aras De Los Olmos dog-friendly?
A: Many trails in Aras De Los Olmos are suitable for dogs, but it's always recommended to check specific route details for any restrictions or requirements, such as leash laws or protected areas. Ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you carry waste bags.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Aras De Los Olmos?
A: Yes, the area offers various trails suitable for families. The easier routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40301447" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arcos de las Salinas – Baños Waterfall loop&lt;/a&gt;, are great for shorter outings with children. The diverse terrain ensures there are options for different family activity levels.

Q: What are the parking options for hikers in Aras De Los Olmos?
A: Parking is generally available in and around the town of Aras De Los Olmos, as well as at designated trailheads for more popular routes. It's advisable to check specific starting points for parking availability, especially for longer or more remote trails.

Q: Is public transport available to reach hiking trailheads?
A: While Aras De Los Olmos is a mountain town, public transport options might be limited for direct access to all trailheads. It's best to research local bus schedules or consider driving to reach the starting points of your chosen hikes.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Aras De Los Olmos?
A: The trails in Aras De Los Olmos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 11 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the tranquility of the natural environment, and the variety of routes available for all skill levels.
tips:
This is the Corcolilla dinosaur footprint site, declared a Site of Cultural Interest with paleontological status in 2006. 
Numerous footprints and traces from various small and large herbivorous and carnivorous dinosaurs dating back 145 million years can be seen.

waypoints.name: Dino Route of Aras de los Olmos
